title: oldenlandia fruticosa
intro: Oldenlandia fruticosa, also known as Phyla fruticosa or Lippia nodiflora, is a perennial herb native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, characterized by its woody stems and small, fragrant flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Oldenlandia fruticosa include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and its leaves and stems can be dried and used in infusions or tinctures for various medicinal purposes.
